title: 'Our infrastructure was designed for a climate that no longer exists': France's unfinished reckoning with increasing heatwaves
subhead: In 2003, an unprecedented heatwave struck France in the middle of August while the government was on vacation. It killed 15,000 people. While this public health shock transformed the alert system and the management of heat-related illnesses, France has failed to learn any lessons regarding its necessary adaptation to climate change.

title: Civilian plane crash kills 11 in France
From France to Italy, inside the counterfeit rings circulating fake banknotes across Europe via social media
'Summer and extreme heat make all the symptoms worse': Coping with a heatwave and mental health conditions
subhead: As temperatures rise, several studies have shown an increase in visits to psychiatric emergency rooms and suicide attempts.

title: 2026 World Cup: In New York, football takes over the city where basketball is king
subhead: The North American World Cup could be on the brink of giving football its rightful place in New York City. Fields are popping up across the city, and an ever-growing number of players are starting to overshadow traditional American sports.

title: 'The King and the Mockingbird': The animated masterpiece that inspired Studio Ghibli
subhead: His film took more than three decades to reach the screen. In 1946, Paul Grimault, then 41, began work on 'Le Roi et l'Oiseau' ('The King and the Mockingbird') with Jacques Prévert writing the screenplay. He finally saw it screened in 1979, at 74. The hand-drawn animation became a cult classic, inspiring generations of viewers and filmmakers.

title: To join the 'system' or overturn it? Far-right leader Bardella's private life and policies raise doubts
subhead: By openly seeking to appeal to economic elites and the right, far-right leader Jordan Bardella breaks with Marine Le Pen and her father's historic 'anti-system' hostility. Some party members question his desire to pursue radical policies.

Sleeping in a cellar, waiting at a funeral home, riding the tram: How the French are improvising to survive the heatwave
subhead: Residents describe the sometimes unusual strategies they have adopted to endure the heat. Their improvised solutions highlight the shortcomings in climate change adaptation.

title: In Ukraine, those who leave and those who stay: Life on hold in Sloviansk and Kramatorsk
subhead: 'Donbas: War of Attrition' (2/2). In this industrial region, 80% of which is occupied by Russian troops, the cities of Sloviansk and Kramatorsk have become the heart of the Ukrainian defensive system. Some of their residents choose to flee to safer areas. Others try to survive as best they can.
byline: Thomas d’Istria and  Virginie NGuyen Hoang

title: In the age of AI, Chinese universities overhaul their curricula
subhead: Universities across China are undergoing a major transformation to align with the economic priorities of the Chinese government and to tackle youth unemployment. To survive, some disciplines, such as foreign languages, now have to attach themselves to hard sciences.
